entries.title: Commentary on Isaiah on isaiah 17:13
entries.excerpt: (Verse 13) But to them, like raging seas, the Lord will rebuke the instigator of their sedition, and He will drive him away from His people. And as dust moved on the mountains, the higher it is, the stronger it is carried away; and as a whirlwind, which rises from the earth, is carried away into the sky by a sudden storm: so also he, being carried away, will be separated from the people of God and will flee, lest he be relegated to the abyss.

entries.title: COMMENTARY ON ISAIAH 5:17.12-14 on isaiah 17:13
entries.excerpt: (Verse 13) But when raging warriors come and inundate my land, then their prince Sennacherib will flee from them, rebuked, and they will be scattered like dust in a plundering storm. As the top of a whirl-wind revolves, so will he be struck by an angel when he approaches Jerusalem to besiege her.

entries.title: Commentary on Isaiah on isaiah 17:13
entries.excerpt: (Verse 13) But when they come raging and flood my land, then their leader Sennacherib will flee, reproached by him, and be scattered like dust carried by the wind, and whirled up into the sky like a storm. Indeed, he will approach Jerusalem, besieging it, but he will be struck down by an angel.

entries.title: Commentary on Isaiah on isaiah 17:13
entries.excerpt:
As to the shout of their fighting, nations shall make a noise, in battle, above: behold the Lord will bring upon them the waters of the river strong and many (Isa 8:7); their voice shall roar like the sea (Jer 6:23); many waters, many peoples (Rev 17).

Second, he threatens destruction. Concerning which he first sets out the destruction itself: but he shall rebuke him, killing his army by an angel, and he shall flee far off, to his own land with only a few men, as it says above (Isa 10:18); you have rebuked the Gentiles (Ps 9:6[5]). Second, a similitude is set out corresponding to the condition of the destruction, for he was proud: he shall be carried away as the dust: like the dust (Ps 1:4), which is driven before the wind; and corresponding to the time: as a whirlwind before a tempest, for by night his army was slain, which he calls a tempest, whose precursor is a whirling of dust by winds, which are the cause of rain: in the night an angel came, and slew the army of the Assyrians (2 Kgs 19:35).
